meta.description: Narrative graph for Rolf's journey to York

backstory.father: Godric of Wessex, a minor thegn who bent the knee to William
backstory.mother: Aelith, died in childbirth with a third son who also died
backstory.childhood: Raised to inherit. Trained with sword and ledger both.
backstory.wound: Edmund's betrayal. The moment Father died, Edmund produced a 'new' will.

backstory.family: The Thornwick Aldrics. Farmers who became fighters when the Normans came.
backstory.grandmother: Wulfhild. She told him stories of the old kings. Died before the Harrying.
backstory.loss: His brother Oswin died at Hastings. His parents in the Harrying.
backstory.why_with_rolf: Rolf spared him at the bridge when he could have killed him. Aldric saw something.
backstory.oath_meaning: An oath is sacred. His father taught him that. Break an oath, break yourself.

backstory.resentment: Father favored Rolf. Everyone favored Rolf.
backstory.justification: The will was real. Father changed his mind. Rolf was too soft for these times.
backstory.current_plan: Curry favor with Sheriff Malet. Become indispensable. Rise.

backstory.position: Sheriff of Yorkshire. One of the Conqueror's men.
backstory.reputation: Fair, for a Norman. But still a Norman.
backstory.concern: The north is restless. Edgar Atheling stirs. Gospatric plots.

backstory.past: Was a reeve before the conquest. Now scrapes by in York.
backstory.knowledge: Knows everyone's business. Information is currency.
backstory.price: Everything has a price. His is survival.

places.name: The Camp
places.description: Three days from York. A clearing in bare woods.

places.name: York
places.description: The great city of the north. Norman garrison. Saxon resentment. Where Edmund is.

places.name: Thornwick
places.description: A village half-emptied by the Harrying. Aldric's family came from here.

places.name: Durham
places.description: The bishop's seat. Another center of power in the north.

places.name: Father's Hall
places.description: Where Rolf grew up. Now Edmund's. Or sold. Rolf doesn't know.

things.name: The Will
things.description: The document Edmund produced. Is it real? Forged? Does it matter now?

things.name: Aldric's Blade
things.description: His father's sword. The only thing left from before.

things.name: Father's Ring
things.description: The ring Father gave Rolf before he died. Edmund doesn't know he has it.

narratives.content: While Father lay dying, Edmund produced a will naming himself sole heir. Everything was taken. The land, the hall, the future.
narratives.voiceText: Three days to York. He's close now.

narratives.content: At the fire on Day 1, Aldric swore an oath: to serve until released, to follow where Rolf leads, to keep faith. He has never broken an oath.
narratives.voiceText: I swore to see Aldric home.

narratives.summary: Rolf swore to reclaim what's his
narratives.content: Before leaving the south, Rolf swore to find Edmund and take back his birthright. By word or by blood.
narratives.voiceText: Find him. Take back what's yours. That's why we came north.

narratives.content: Same father. Same blood. That makes the betrayal worse. Or does it make forgiveness possible?

narratives.summary: Thornwick reminds Aldric of his loss
narratives.content: Aldric knew a family in Thornwick before the Harrying. They're gone now. He won't say more.
narratives.voiceText: He hasn't spoken since Thornwick. The village reminded him of something.

narratives.content: William's response to northern rebellion. Villages burned. Crops destroyed. Thousands starved. Aldric lost everyone.
narratives.voiceText: The north remembers what was done.

narratives.summary: Edmund is in York, seeking favor
narratives.content: Word is Edmund has been seen in York, speaking with men close to Sheriff Malet. He's building something.
narratives.voiceText: He's in York. Building alliances. Growing stronger.

narratives.summary: Rolf spared Aldric at the bridge
narratives.content: They met as enemies. Rolf had Aldric beaten. Could have killed him. Didn't. Aldric has wondered why ever since.
narratives.voiceText: You could have killed me. You didn't.

narratives.content: Rolf believes the will is false. Father would never have disinherited him. But proving it is another matter.
narratives.voiceText: Father would never. The will is a lie.

narratives.summary: Edmund believes he deserves the inheritance
narratives.content: To Edmund, the will is real and just. Rolf was soft, unsuited to hard times. Edmund saved the family legacy.
Malet needs men who can keep the peace. Those who prove useful rise. Those who cause trouble disappear.
narratives.voiceText: The sheriff rewards those who serve. And destroys those who don't.

connections.nature: Aldric serves Rolf. Bound by oath. Growing into trust.
Brothers. Rolf believes Edmund betrayed him. Hunting him.
Fears Rolf. Knows he's coming. Preparing.
Edmund is cultivating Malet. Seeking protection and advancement.
Aldric's family came from here. They're gone now. The Harrying.

tensions.description: Rolf is three days from York. Edmund is there. Confrontation is inevitable.
Aldric is bound by oath, but the north's wounds are his wounds. What if Rolf's path leads to more suffering?
Edmund is the enemy. Edmund is also blood. Can Rolf kill his brother? Should he?
Both brothers believe they're right. The truth may be more complicated than either knows.

setups.description: Rolf will reach York soon. The confrontation with Edmund approaches.
Aldric's silence about Thornwick. There's more there.
setups.payoff: Aldric reveals what happened to his family
